content: Checkpoint inhibitor selection depends on cancer type, biomarkers, and prior therapy. Pembrolizumab approved for MSI-H/dMMR tumors regardless of origin (tumor-agnostic); also first-line for NSCLC with PD-L1 ≥50%, head/neck cancer, melanoma. Nivolumab + ipilimumab combination shows superior outcomes in melanoma, renal cell carcinoma, mesothelioma but increased toxicity. Dosing: pembrolizumab 200mg IV q3w or 400mg q6w; nivolumab 240mg q2w or 480mg q4w; continued until progression or 2 years maximum in adjuvant setting. Unique response patterns: pseudoprogression (initial enlargement before response) occurs in ~10%; immune-related RECIST (iRECIST) accounts for this. Hyperprogression (accelerated growth) rare but recognized. Duration: metastatic disease typically continued until progression; adjuvant therapy fixed duration (1 year). Combination with chemotherapy enhances efficacy in many cancers (lung, triple-negative breast).
